# @json-express/middleware-validation — AI Context

Input validation middleware (Zod underneath). Enforces the `validation` blocks declared in models — installing this package is what makes them active.

## Install — that is the whole integration
```
npm i @json-express/middleware-validation
```
Discovery is automatic. **Never import zod schemas into an app file or register anything.**

## Configuration (.env)
No keys — everything is declared in `models/`.

## What it unlocks (all in `models/`)
```ts
export default defineModel({
  fields: { id: types.id(), email: types.string({ unique: true }) },
  validation: {
    create: {},                       // {} = auto-baseline derived from fields
    update: { body: (base) => base.partial() },
    list:   { query: myQueryValidator },
  },
});
```
- Invalid requests get HTTP 400 with structured field errors before any handler runs.
- Custom endpoints take their own `validation` next to the handler: `{ handler, validation }`.
- Without this package installed the `validation` blocks are inert metadata (documented in docs-swagger either way).
